# Best Practices Compendium

## lrv-contrast-visual-impairment-built-environment|VIS
**Updated:** 2026-03-18 11:30  **Evidence tier:** 3-4
**Consensus finding:** 30 LRV point-difference is the minimum floor across UK, US, and Australian standards; best practice for critical navigation and signage is Michelson K>=0.7 per DIN 32975:2009 (mandatory for public buildings in Germany per DIN 18040-3:2014).
**Range:** Minimum >=30 LRV point-difference (BS 8300); best practice K>=0.7 Michelson (DIN 32975) for critical elements; >=60 LRV point-difference for transport signage (UN ECE)
**Jurisdictions confirmed:** UK (BS 8300), DE (DIN 32975; DIN 18040-3), NO (TEK17), US (ADA), AU (AS 1428.1)
**Early-close:** yes  **Thin/No-data:** SV (references EN framework only)
**Key sources:** DIN 32975:2009; DIN 18040-3:2014; BS 8300-2:2018; TEK17 s.12-16; Dain 2022 (Clin Exp Optom)
**Divergent findings:** DIN 32975 K>=0.7 (Michelson) vs BS 8300 >=30 LRV point-difference -- not contradictory; K>=0.7 typically requires >=40+ LRV point-difference in mid-tone contexts, representing a higher standard

## corridor-width-accessible-route-public-buildings|MOB
**Updated:** 2026-03-18 11:30  **Evidence tier:** 4
**Consensus finding:** >=1200mm is a compliance minimum (UK/US); >=1500mm is best practice for primary public building routes per DIN 18040-1 and ISO 21542; >=1800mm is ideal per TEK17 s.8-6 (two wheelchairs passing).
**Range:** Minimum >=1200mm; best practice >=1500mm; ideal >=1800mm
**Jurisdictions confirmed:** DE (1500mm -- DIN 18040-1 s.4.2), NO (1800mm/1400mm -- TEK17 s.8-6), UK (1200mm + passing places -- BS 8300), US (1220mm -- ADA)
**Early-close:** yes  **Thin/No-data:** SV
**Key sources:** DIN 18040-1:2010 s.4.2; TEK17 s.8-6; ISO 21542:2021; BS 8300-2:2018
**Divergent findings:** DE/NO substantially more stringent than UK/US minimums; guidebook >=1200mm is at UK/US minimum only

## grab-bar-height-accessible-bathroom|MOB
**Updated:** 2026-03-18 11:30  **Evidence tier:** 3-4
**Consensus finding:** Wall-mounted horizontal grab bars at 800-900mm AFF is supported by BS 8300, ADA, and Norwegian standards. DIN 18040 folding-grip (Stuetzklappgriff) position at 740-760mm AFF is a different product type; functional distinction not a spec conflict. Bilateral specification is evidence-supported by Levine (2023) and Lee (2019).
**Range:** Wall-mounted horizontal: 800-900mm AFF; DIN 18040 folding grip: 740-760mm AFF; DIN 18040 shower horizontal: 850mm AFF
**Jurisdictions confirmed:** UK (BS 8300), US (ADA 840-915mm), DE (DIN 18040 with product distinction), NO (NS 11001/TEK17)
**Early-close:** yes  **Thin/No-data:** SV
**Key sources:** BS 8300-2:2018; ADA s.604.5; DIN 18040-1:2010; Levine IC et al. (2023) Hum Factors 65(8):1821-1829 doi:10.1177/00187208211059860; Lee YS et al. (2019) Disabil Rehabil Assist Technol
**Divergent findings:** DIN 18040 WC folding grip 740-760mm vs wall-mounted standard 800-900mm -- functional difference (product type), not spec conflict

## visual-alarm-device-seizure-risk-photosensitive-epilepsy|NEU
**Updated:** 2026-03-18 11:30  **Evidence tier:** 3-4
**Consensus finding:** BS EN 54-23 VADs operate at 0.5-2 Hz flash rate; fire alarm strobes are documented PSE triggers (Epilepsy Foundation 2024); no jurisdiction in any searched language specifies non-strobe VAD alternatives for spaces where photosensitive epilepsy populations are primary occupants. This is a systemic cross-jurisdictional gap.
**Range:** Flash rate 0.5-2 Hz (EN 54-23 standard) -- clinical risk: lower than 3-30 Hz peak seizure zone but individual sensitivity varies; not risk-free
**Jurisdictions confirmed:** UK, DE, NO -- all EN 54-23 compliant; all confirm gap
**Early-close:** yes  **Thin/No-data:** SV, NO (no PSE-specific guidance in either)
**Key sources:** BS EN 54-23; Epilepsy Foundation (2024); PMC (2025) PSE guidelines gap analysis; DIN VDE 0833-2
**Divergent findings:** None -- gap is universal across all searched jurisdictions
**Gap registered:** P1 systemic -- no jurisdiction specifies non-strobe VAD alternative for PSE-occupant spaces
